examples.title: Bit Stuffing Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to insert stuffed bits in data transmission
examples.code:
PROCEDURE BitStuff(data):
 stuffedData = empty string
 consecutiveOnes = 0
 
 FOR each bit in data:
 IF bit == '1':
 consecutiveOnes = consecutiveOnes + 1
 ELSE:
 consecutiveOnes = 0
 
 stuffedData = stuffedData + bit
 
 IF consecutiveOnes == 5:
 stuffedData = stuffedData + '0'
 consecutiveOnes = 0
 
 RETURN stuffedData

PROCEDURE BitDestuff(stuffedData):
 data = empty string
 consecutiveOnes = 0
 
 FOR each bit in stuffedData:
 IF bit == '1':
 consecutiveOnes = consecutiveOnes + 1
 ELSE:
 consecutiveOnes = 0
 
 data = data + bit
 
 IF consecutiveOnes == 5:
 skip next bit (the stuffed '0')
 consecutiveOnes = 0
 
 RETURN data
examples.explanation: Bit stuffing prevents the flag pattern (01111110) from appearing in the data by inserting a '0' after every five consecutive '1' bits. The receiver removes these stuffed bits to recover original data.

examples.title: Stop-and-Wait ARQ Protocol
examples.description: Implementation of Stop-and-Wait sender and receiver
examples.code:
// SENDER
WHILE data to send:
 frame = createFrame(seqNum)
 send(frame)
 startTimer(frame)
 
 WAIT for ACK or timeout:
 IF ACK received:
 stopTimer(frame)
 seqNum = 1 - seqNum // toggle
 getNextData()
 
 ELSE IF timeout:
 // retransmit same frame
 send(frame)
 startTimer(frame)

// RECEIVER
WHILE true:
 frame = receive()
 
 IF frame.seqNum == expectedSeqNum:
 deliverData(frame.data)
 sendACK(expectedSeqNum)
 expectedSeqNum = 1 - expectedSeqNum
 
 ELSE:
 // duplicate or out-of-order
 sendACK(1 - expectedSeqNum) // ACK last received
examples.explanation: Sender sends one frame and waits for ACK before sending next. If timeout occurs, it retransmits. Receiver delivers only if sequence number matches expected, otherwise sends duplicate ACK.

examples.title: CRC Calculation
examples.description: CRC-32 polynomial division for error detection
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CalculateCRC(data, generator):
 // generator for CRC-32: 0x04C11DB7
 // Append r-1 zeros to data (r = generator degree)
 paddedData = data + zeros(generator.degree - 1)
 
 // Perform polynomial division using XOR
 FOR i = 0 TO paddedData.length - generator.degree:
 IF paddedData[i] == '1':
 FOR j = 0 TO generator.degree:
 paddedData[i+j] = paddedData[i+j] XOR generator[j]
 
 // Remainder is the CRC value
 CRC = last (generator.degree - 1) bits of paddedData
 RETURN CRC

PROCEDURE VerifyCRC(receivedData, generator):
 CRC = CalculateCRC(receivedData, generator)
 IF CRC == 0:
 RETURN true // No error detected
 ELSE:
 RETURN false // Error detected
examples.explanation: CRC uses polynomial division to detect errors. The sender computes remainder using generator polynomial and appends it. Receiver divides the received frame by same polynomial - if remainder is zero, no error.
